subject: Geography
title: Caves in Warfare
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Caves in Warfare.

hook.text: During WWII, Britain stored art treasures in Welsh slate mines to protect them from bombs.

buildup.text: Caves have served as fortresses, hospitals, and ammunition stores throughout military history.

discovery.text: The Viet Cong built 250 km of tunnels at Cù Chi, hiding entire divisions underground.

twist.text: Tora Bora's caves in Afghanistan resisted the world's most powerful military for weeks.

climax.text: Underground, even the smallest force can resist the largest army—caves equalize power.

punchline.text: In war, going underground is the great equalizer.

quiz.question: What did the Viet Cong build at Cù Chi?
